```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  x <- sort(sample(-3:6,4)); raw <- sample(1:8,4,replace=TRUE); p <- raw/sum(raw)
  ans <- sum(x*p); vals <- round(c(ans,mean(x),sum(x),sum(p),sum(abs(x)*p)),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals); xt <- paste(x,collapse=", "); pt <- paste(sprintf("%.3f",p),collapse=", ")
```

Question
========
Uma variável discreta assume valores $(`r xt`)$ com probabilidades $(`r pt`)$. Qual é $E(X)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A esperança é a média ponderada:
$$E(X)=\sum_xxp_X(x)=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S8Q1_esperanca_discreta
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
